title: Chettinad Mutton Kuzhambu

subs.name: 700 g chicken — reduce pressure-cook to 3 whistles
1.5 tbsp tamarind paste in 150 ml water
80 ml coconut milk added with the stock at step 6
coconut oil for a different but authentic flavour

steps.text: Soak tamarind in 200 ml warm water; squeeze and strain. Dry roast fennel seeds and black peppercorns in the kadai for 2 minutes on low. Transfer to a mixie with the grated coconut and 4 tablespoons of water; grind to a smooth paste.

Heat 2 tablespoons of gingelly oil in a pressure cooker over medium heat. Add star anise, cinnamon, cloves and mustard seeds. When they splutter, add half the shallots and fry until golden. Add the mutton, turmeric, a pinch of chilli powder, salt and 150 ml water. Seal and pressure-cook on high until 5 whistles, then lower to medium and cook 10 minutes more. Allow pressure to release naturally.
Heat remaining 2 tablespoons of gingelly oil in the kadai. Add the remaining shallots and curry leaves. Fry until the shallots are deep golden and slightly crispy — about 7 minutes.
Add ginger-garlic paste and fry 2 minutes. Add tomatoes with a pinch of salt and cook until they collapse and the oil separates.
Lower the flame. Add red chilli powder and coriander powder. Stir for 30 seconds, then add the tamarind water. Bring to a boil.
Add the pressure-cooked mutton with all its stock. Stir well, cover and simmer on medium for 12 minutes until the gravy deepens.

Stir in the coconut-pepper paste. Simmer uncovered for 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the gravy is thick and glossy with oil floating on top. Taste and adjust salt. Rest 10 minutes before serving.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
critical_checks: Use an appropriate food thermometer and safe doneness guidance for the specific cut of meat.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: mutton → 700 g chicken — reduce pressure-cook to 3 whistles; tamarind → 1.5 tbsp tamarind paste in 150 ml water; coconut → 80 ml coconut milk added with the stock at step 6; gingelly-oil → coconut oil for a different but authentic flavour
